patches.find: all methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone. Spacecraft-based measurements
patches.replace: all methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone. The cleanest independence test is the raw Gaia astrometric residuals — the annual sinusoidal position signature has amplitude that correlates with spectroscopic-class distance estimates (which set absolute magnitudes from spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation), confirming that the 1/d pattern is in the geometry of the data and not an artifact of the photometric calibration chain. Spacecraft-based measurements
patches.rationale: EXP-287: preempts period-luminosity circularity objection by inserting one sentence pointing to spectroscopic-class distance estimates as an independence axis. ISS-1847 carry-forward from WIN-017.c3 hole #3.

patches.find: appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime (no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly)
patches.replace: are static constants with no live data fetch at runtime (verified 2026-04-25, curmudgeon WIN-045.c4 code-tag audit)
patches.rationale: WIN-045.c4 moderate hole: the hedge 'no independent code audit has been performed' is contradicted by the curmudgeon c4 review which DID perform a code audit and verified the monitoring=hardcoded tag. Removes self-contradiction and tightens the prose.
